title: festuca bosniaca subsp. pirinensis
intro: Festuca bosniaca subsp. pirinensis, also known as Pirin fescue, is a subspecies of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass native to the Pirin Mountains in Bulgaria and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Festuca bosniaca subsp. pirinensis include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, and are often used to support respiratory health and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
